title: Traits 1
description: Traits define shared behavior. A trait tells the compiler about functionality a type must provide.
instructions: Implement the AppendBar trait for the String type.
hints: The method takes `self` by value, meaning it takes ownership of the String.
You can use the `+` operator or `format!` to concatenate strings.
explanation: Traits are like interfaces in other languages. You define the method signatures in the trait, then provide implementations for specific types. The `impl TraitName for Type` syntax implements the trait. Methods that take `self` by value consume the original value and return a new one.
